@graph.rdfs:label: seed number_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Scanning seed number using an X-ray CT scanner, at 220kV voltage and 6mA current, utilizing a 0.5mm copper filter for image acquisition at a resolution of 84µm. The specimen stage rotated 220 degrees with a rotation step increment of 0.15 degrees. Throughout the 7-minute scanning duration, 1500 projections were collected at an exposure time of 250 ms each. Correction and reconstruction were carried out using the Fraunhofer EZRT CTProcessingNet, following the approach described by Schmidt et al. (2020). Reconstructed 3D grey-scale volume, with a depth of 16 bits, was then segmented into 30 individual 3D volumes using the EarSTubesSegmentation algorithm.

@graph.rdfs:label: spike seed ratio surface to volume_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Scanning spike seed ratio surface to volume using an X-ray CT scanner, at 220kV voltage and 6mA current, utilizing a 0.5mm copper filter for image acquisition at a resolution of 84µm. The specimen stage rotated 220 degrees with a rotation step increment of 0.15 degrees. Throughout the 7-minute scanning duration, 1500 projections were collected at an exposure time of 250 ms each. Correction and reconstruction were carried out using the Fraunhofer EZRT CTProcessingNet, following the approach described by Schmidt et al. (2020). Reconstructed 3D grey-scale volume, with a depth of 16 bits, was then segmented into 30 individual 3D volumes using the EarSTubesSegmentation algorithm. Spike, automatic wheat seed segmentation and measurements were conducted using the EarS algorithm. Spikelet information, including the number of spikelets per spike and the number of grains per spikelet, estimated using a method described by Zhou et al. (2021), with modifications implemented in a Python 3.0 environment.

@graph.rdfs:label: shoot fresh weight_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the total weight of the above ground plant material

@graph.rdfs:label: shoot dry weight_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the total weight of the dried above ground plant material

@graph.rdfs:label: root fresh weight_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the total weight of below ground plant material

@graph.rdfs:label: root dry weight_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the total weight of the dried below ground plant material

@graph.rdfs:label: total fresh weight_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the total weight of above and below ground plant material

@graph.rdfs:label: total dry weight_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the total weight of dried above and below ground plant material

@graph.rdfs:label: harvest index_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as ratio of Yield to Biomass

@graph.rdfs:label: soil nitrate concentration_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Short method description including refs if available. Need to talk to Bettina about these.

@graph.rdfs:label: soil ammonia concentration_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Short method description including refs if available. Need to talk to Bettina about these.

@graph.rdfs:label: soil nitrogen concentration_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Short method description including refs if available. Need to talk to Bettina about these.

@graph.rdfs:label: projected shoot area _method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the sum of the number of plant pixels typically from 3 camera views (LemnaTec Scanalyzer 3D) comprising two side views and aview from above, described by Brien et al. (2020) (https://doi.org/10.1186/s13007-020-00577-6), employing the R package growthPheno (Brien, 2021c) http://cran.at.r-project.org/package=growthPheno)

@graph.rdfs:label: absolute projected shoot area growth rates_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The absolute PSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive PSA values, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - PSA 'Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: relative projected shoot area growth rates_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The relative PSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive ln(PSA) values, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - PSA 'Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: daily water use_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Daily water use (WUR mL/day) was computed for each pot using the Droughtspotter gravimetric platform (Droughtspotter, Phenospex, Heerlen, Netherlands) (https://phenospex.com/products/plant-phenotyping/drought-control-platform/)

@graph.rdfs:label: total water use_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Total Water Use (TWU) was defined for each plant as the sum of all daily WUR values (cross-reference Traits WUR - 'daily water use' [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: water use index_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Water Use Index (WUI kpixels/mL) was calculated as the ratio of sPSA 50 (proxy for final biomass) to TWU. Higher values of WUI correspond to higher water efficiency (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID], TWU Total Water Use [Trait ID]), biomass [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: smoothed projected shoot area_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The smoothed PSA (sPSA kpixels) is calculated by smoothing the PSA using splines as described by Brien et al. (2020). (cross-reference traits - PSA 'Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID]), described by Brien et al. (2020) (https://doi.org/10.1186/s13007-020-00577-6), employing the R package growthPheno (Brien, 2021c) http://cran.at.r-project.org/package=growthPheno)

@graph.rdfs:label: absolute smoothed projected shoot area growth rates_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The absolute sPSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive sPSA values, respectively, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: relative smoothed projected shoot area growth rate_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The relative sPSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive ln(PSA) values, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: projected shoot area for a single time point_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Calculated as the sum of the number of plant pixels from 3 camera views (LemnaTec Scanalyzer 3D) comprising two side views and aview from above, described by Brien et al. (2020) (https://doi.org/10.1186/s13007-020-00577-6), employing the R package growthPheno (Brien, 2021c) http://cran.at.r-project.org/package=growthPheno)

@graph.rdfs:label: absolute projected shoot area growth rates for a single time point_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The absolute PSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive PSA values, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: relative projected shoot area growth rate for a single time point_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The relative PSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive ln(PSA) values, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: absolute projected shoot area growth rates over a time interval_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The absolute PSA growth rates were calculated by differencing PSA values at two different time points, t1 and t2, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: relative projected shoot area growth rate over a time interval_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The relative PSA growth rates were calculated by differencing ln(PSA) values at two different time points, t1 and t2, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: smoothed projected shoot area for a single time point_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The smoothed PSA (sPSA) is calculated by smoothing the PSA using splines as described by Brien et al. (2020). (cross-reference traits - PSA 'Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID]), described by Brien et al. (2020) (https://doi.org/10.1186/s13007-020-00577-6), employing the R package growthPheno (Brien, 2021c) http://cran.at.r-project.org/package=growthPheno)

@graph.rdfs:label: absolute smoothed projected shoot area growth rates for a single time point_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The absolute sPSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive sPSA values and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: relative smoothed projected shoot area growth rate for a single time point_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The relative sPSA growth rates were calculated by differencing consecutive ln(sPSA) values, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: absolute smoothed projected shoot area growth rates over a time interval_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The absolute sPSA growth rates were calculated by differencing sPSA values at two different time points, t1 and t2, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])

@graph.rdfs:label: relative smoothed projected shoot area growth rate over a time interval_method
@graph.rdfs:comment: The relative sPSA growth rates were calculated by differencing ln(sPSA) values at two different time points, t1 and t2, and dividing by the time differences. (cross-reference traits - sPSA 'smoothed Projected Shoot Area [Trait ID])
